title: waldsteinia fragarioides subsp. doniana
intro: Waldsteinia fragarioides subsp. doniana, commonly known as the Don's wood-sorrel or Don's creeping wood-sorrel, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the saxifrage family. It is known for its edible and medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Waldsteinia fragarioides subsp. doniana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies such as salves and ointments to promote wound healing and skin health.
